title: Rust-native LifeOS application boundary and API family defined
objective: Define the stable Rust-native application, state, command, event, plugin, policy, and data APIs that presentation clients consume without owning LifeOS logic. Every user-visible capability resolves through one of these seven governed API families; business logic and durable authority stay inside the Rust-native application core (the governed bounded contexts) and never migrate into a replaceable client; offline, error, rollback, and proof behavior are specified per family and globally.
verification_gate: Every user-visible capability resolves through stable governed APIs; business logic and authority stay outside replaceable clients; offline, error, rollback, and proof behavior are specified.
supporting_sources: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv
src/lifeos/CLAUDE.md (existing Tauri/Vue app surfaces: ai_complete, ai_provider_get/set, db_health, db_migrate, calm error strings, six workspaces + settings)
input_note: Inputs per task_graph.source.csv: LIFEOS-010 (coherent replaceable interface layer over a Rust-native substrate), CONSOLIDATE-001 (the 15 bounded contexts and their published contracts that own all durable authority), the existing Tauri/Vue app, and governed domain contracts. This family does not invent capabilities; it exposes the already-governed CONSOLIDATE-001 contract surface through a stable seven-family API and pins the client/authority boundary.
governing_decision: STORE-001 adopt-intended-tiered-architecture. BC-UI-PRESENTATION owns no durable macro-state; PostgreSQL + RuVector remains the single durable writer; redb is the transient/WAL tier; clients are read-only sinks that dispatch governed commands. The API boundary enforces the single-durable-writer invariant by construction: no API family lets a client become a durable authority.

application_boundary.principle: The UI is not the system. The Rust-native application core is the system; presentation clients are replaceable skins over a fixed seven-family API. This realizes LIFEOS-010 (separate Rust-native substrate from replaceable presentation) and the blueprint statement that LifeOS is a Mission Control UI that renders swarm status by reading the same memory-mapped redb state and PostgreSQL via UDS/IPC as a read-only sink.

rust_native_core.identity: The 15 CONSOLIDATE-001 bounded contexts compiled into the single-application host, minus BC-UI-PRESENTATION which is the client role itself.
owns: all business logic (routing, forecasting, ingestion, bridging, cognition, policy, verification)
all durable authority (STORE-001: PostgreSQL+RuVector durable macro-state, redb transient tier, AgentDB per-agent cognition, OS keyring secrets, envctl projections)
all command validation, policy enforcement, proof emission, and rollback execution
rust_native_core.exposes_only: the seven governed API families defined in api_families; nothing else crosses the boundary.

replaceable_clients.current: Vue3 + Vite + Pinia + vue-router + Tauri2 shell (BC-UI-PRESENTATION PresentationShell + UDSClient).
replaceable_clients.future_approved: desktop-first, then approved mobile, glasses, and ambient surfaces (LIFEOS-010; decomposed further by EXPERIENCE-002). Any client is swappable without moving logic because the seven-family API is the only contract.
replaceable_clients.presentation_groupings_no_authority: The six existing workspaces (ai, gaming, work, personal, home, media) plus the /settings context are navigation containers only. They group and render governed capabilities; they hold no business logic and no durable state. A grouping is a view over the API families, never an owner.

clients_may: render governed state and data projections (State API, Data API)
dispatch typed commands to the owning context (Command API)
subscribe to server-pushed events and streams (Event API)
request a policy verdict before offering an action (Policy API)
list and invoke governed plugins through the protocol (Plugin API)
read application lifecycle/health and negotiate API version (Application API)
display proof references and error strings surfaced by the core
clients_may_not: mutate durable governed state directly (must go through Command API into the owning context)
embed business rules, routing, forecasting, verification, or migration logic
become a second durable writer for any STORE-001 macro-state class (single-durable-writer invariant)
hold raw secrets (references only; BC-SECRET keeps keys in the OS keyring)
bypass Policy API enforcement or forge a proof/witness chain
reconstruct authority locally (e.g., write directly to redb-durable or to PostgreSQL) outside the governed API
client_contract.authority_containment_by_context: Enumerated in api_family_contract_coverage and store_001_alignment: every durable state class stays with its STORE-001 canonical owner behind the API boundary.

api_families.name: Application Lifecycle & Capability Handshake API
api_families.purpose: Boot, health, shutdown, and capability/version negotiation for the whole application. A client attaches, discovers which API families and versions the core supports, receives lifecycle/navigation intents, and probes health. Owns nothing domain-specific; it is the front door.
api_families.transport: Tauri IPC command channel + native menu events (e.g. lifeos:navigate); available before any domain API.

operations.name: boot/init
operations.routes_to: setup orchestrator: Storage::new -> migrate() -> accounts::migrate_from_json (from CLAUDE.md storage init sequence)
operations.authority_owner: Rust-native core; refuses to start with calm error on failure

operations.name: shutdown
operations.routes_to: graceful teardown (flush redb WAL forward before exit)

api_families.authority_location: Rust-native core owns boot ordering and refuses to start on storage-init failure (calm string 'LifeOS couldn't initialize storage at <path> — see logs.').
api_families.offline_behavior: Boot proceeds; db_health returns status='degraded' when applied_migrations < embedded count; client shown calm degraded banner, never blocked from local reads.
api_families.error_behavior: Init failure -> calm sentence-case string; app refuses to start rather than start half-initialized.
api_families.rollback_behavior: Not a mutation surface; the task-level rollback (restore archived interface-boundary plan, application source unchanged) applies here.
api_families.proof_behavior: Exposes db_health {status, db_path, applied_migrations, last_migration_version, schema_version} as a boot-time proof handle.
api_families.stability: v0; additive-only; handshake lets old clients keep working when new families are added.

api_families.name: Governed State Read API
api_families.purpose: Read-only projections of governed application/UI state: workspace/section/item ordering, swarm status snapshots, temporal-forecast state, hardware inventory, evidence corpus, prompt registry, task/bead boards, storage health. No mutation path. This is the client's window onto the current governed state.
api_families.transport: UDS / Tauri IPC read calls + shared memory-mapped redb reads (blueprint: LifeOS reads the same redb files agents write).

operations.name: get_swarm_status
operations.routes_to: BC-ORCHESTRATION SwarmRouter + BC-INGEST ScratchpadBuffer (redb) + BC-COGNITION AgentRuntime

operations.name: get_task_board
operations.authority_owner: subordinate task store; provenance references BC-PERSIST

operations.name: get_workspace_layout
operations.routes_to: core projection of persisted keys (activeId, sectionByWs, orders) per CLAUDE.md persistence whitelist
operations.authority_owner: PostgreSQL-backed via BC-PERSIST for durable prefs

api_families.authority_location: All reads are projections; the client cannot write. Durable state stays with each STORE-001 owner. redb reads are the transient tier only.
api_families.offline_behavior: Reads degrade to last-known redb-buffered/cached snapshot when PostgreSQL is offline; the client renders a 'stale as of <t>' marker. redb-backed status keeps flowing because it is local (mmap/UDS).
api_families.error_behavior: Unavailable projection returns a typed empty/stale result with a calm reason string; never a partial/guessed value.
api_families.proof_behavior: State projections that reflect a settled durable fact carry the source provenance reference (module_path / PostgreSQL row / witness root) so a client can show where a value came from.
api_families.stability: v0; additive fields only; removed fields go through deprecation.

api_families.name: Governed Command (Mutation) API
api_families.purpose: The single mutation entry point. A client dispatches a typed command; the core validates it through the Policy API, executes it inside the owning bounded context under that context's authority, and returns a typed result plus a proof and rollback handle. The client never mutates governed state itself.
api_families.transport: Tauri IPC invoke (e.g. invoke('ai_complete', {prompt, source})) / UDS request-response.

operations.name: ai_complete
operations.routes_to: BC-COGNITION AgentRuntime via BC-ORCHESTRATION RuvLTRA routing; Tauri host -> Rust -> provider; Vitest/browser keeps canned-reply path
operations.authority_owner: AgentDB (.rvf) cognition + provider; keys from BC-SECRET keyring

operations.name: set_ai_provider
operations.routes_to: core provider selection; key resolution BC-SECRET
operations.authority_owner: OS keyring (BC-SECRET); provider choice persisted per whitelist

operations.name: start_ingest
operations.routes_to: BC-INGEST IngestPipeline (Nushell parse -> redb buffer -> BlobStore capture)
operations.authority_owner: redb transient tier; durable blobs cut over to PostgreSQL via verified BlobStore SHA-256 parity (STORE-001)

operations.name: materialize_release
operations.routes_to: BC-BRIDGE Projection (PostgreSQL -> filesystem via module_path) then BC-FOUNDATION PortableBuild (musl static)
operations.authority_owner: envctl projection is read-only from PostgreSQL; PostgreSQL is source of truth

operations.name: route_task
operations.authority_owner: orchestration reads BC-PERSIST; commits via COW branch merge

operations.name: network_control
operations.authority_owner: durable home/network state persisted to BC-PERSIST (single writer)

operations.name: set_secret_reference
operations.authority_owner: OS keyring / external vault; PostgreSQL stores provenance references, never raw secrets

operations.name: task_op
operations.authority_owner: subordinate task store; provenance to BC-PERSIST

api_families.authority_location: The command router validates (Policy API), then the OWNING context performs the write under its own STORE-001 authority. The client holds no write capability; it holds a command result + proof_ref + rollback_handle.
api_families.offline_behavior: Commands requiring a durable write while PostgreSQL is offline are accepted into the redb application-level WAL and forward-flushed by envctl on reconnect (forward-only; blueprint redb WAL + STORE-001 invariant). The client is told 'queued, will sync' — it does not itself hold the durable truth. Commands that cannot be safely queued (e.g. release build) are refused with a calm reason.
api_families.error_behavior: Every command returns a typed Result; on reject the core surfaces a calm second-person string (e.g. 'LifeOS couldn't reach the AI provider right now — try again.'). No raw provider/DB error reaches the client.
api_families.rollback_behavior: Each command returns a rollback_handle bound to the owning context's mechanism (COW staging-branch discard, idempotent migration re-run, BlobStore SHA-256 parity cutover reversal with redb retained, meta git snapshot restore). The client may REQUEST rollback; the core EXECUTES it in the owning context.
api_families.proof_behavior: Every governed mutation emits a SHAKE256 Cryptographic Witness Chain rooted in a PostgreSQL source vector; the command result carries proof_ref. Causal-graph verification rejects any claim lacking a historical root, so a client cannot forge a mutation proof.
api_families.stability: v0; command names + payload schemas are the stable contract; additive-only; Vitest/browser dual-mode path (window.__TAURI__ guard) preserved.

api_families.name: Event & Stream Subscription API
api_families.purpose: Server-pushed, read-only streams: real-time swarm/agent status, ingestion progress, build logs, AI token streams, temporal-forecast updates, and policy/MinCut/merge verdicts. The client subscribes and renders; it originates no authority.
api_families.transport: UDS / Tauri event channel + shared memory-mapped redb tailing (blueprint: render swarm status by reading the same redb file agents write).
api_families.authority_location: Push-only; a subscription cannot mutate. Streams are projections of governed state or of context verdicts.
api_families.offline_behavior: redb-sourced streams (swarm status, ingest progress) keep flowing while PostgreSQL is offline because they read the local mmap tier; PostgreSQL-sourced streams pause and resume with a gap marker on reconnect.
api_families.error_behavior: Dropped subscription -> typed 'stream_interrupted' event with a calm reason; the client re-subscribes; no silent gaps.
api_families.rollback_behavior: N/A (read-only); rollback of an underlying mutation surfaces as a compensating event on the stream.
api_families.proof_behavior: Verdict events (MinCut isolation, merge-gate result) are proof-carrying: each references the witness/causal-edge basis so the client can display why an agent was severed or a merge blocked.
api_families.stability: v0; event types are additive; a client ignores unknown event types safely.

api_families.name: Plugin & Extension Protocol API
api_families.purpose: Expose governed extensions: typed subprocess plugins over MessagePack (meta plugin protocol) and Nushell ingestion plugins (nu_plugin CodeDB). A client can list registered plugins and invoke governed plugin operations; the protocol, capability gating, and plugin authority live server-side.
api_families.transport: MessagePack over subprocess stdio (blueprint: native Nushell plugins stream structured data via MessagePack, avoiding JSON); meta_plugin_protocol::run_plugin.

operations.name: invoke_plugin
operations.routes_to: BC-CONTROL-PLANE PluginProtocol -> typed subprocess; BC-INGEST for nu_plugin CodeDB ingestion
operations.authority_owner: plugin subprocess under core-granted capability; ingestion writes to redb buffer then envctl bridge to PostgreSQL

api_families.authority_location: Plugins run as core-hosted subprocesses with core-granted capabilities; a client cannot grant itself plugin capability or bypass the MessagePack protocol. nu_plugin ingestion never becomes a durable authority — it buffers into redb and envctl bridges forward to PostgreSQL (STORE-001).
api_families.offline_behavior: nu_plugin ingestion continues into the redb buffer while PostgreSQL is offline; the envctl bridge forward-flushes on reconnect. Other plugins report degraded per their own contract.
api_families.error_behavior: Plugin crash/timeout -> typed 'plugin_failed' with a calm reason and the plugin id; the core isolates the subprocess; the client is not exposed to raw stderr.
api_families.rollback_behavior: A plugin-initiated governed write is rolled back through the same owning-context mechanism as any command (e.g. ingested blobs discarded before envctl bridge, or COW branch discard).
api_families.proof_behavior: Plugin-produced ingested code carries BLAKE3 dedup hashes and, once bridged, SHA-256 content-addressed identity; witness chains bind the resulting rows to source vectors.
api_families.stability: v0; MessagePack schema versioned; plugin capability grants are explicit and additive.

api_families.name: Policy, Capability-Gating & Verification API
api_families.purpose: Enforcement seam that keeps authority server-side. A client asks 'may I dispatch command X on target Y?' and the core returns an allow/deny verdict; the core also enforces the same policy internally on every command (a client cannot bypass by calling the Command API directly). Fronts the swarm immune system, witness-chain verification, and merge gates.
api_families.transport: Tauri IPC / UDS request-response; also enforced inline on every Command API call (PreToolUse-style denial).
api_families.consumed_by: every replaceable client; also invoked internally by API-COMMAND and API-PLUGIN

operations.name: verify_witness
operations.routes_to: BC-INTEGRITY WitnessChain (SHAKE256) + causal-graph verification over BC-PERSIST

api_families.authority_location: Policy verdicts are advisory to the client but binding in the core: even if a client offers a denied action, the Command API re-checks and refuses. Authority to allow/deny lives in BC-INTEGRITY, never in the client.
api_families.offline_behavior: Policy evaluation is local to the core and continues offline; witness-chain verification against PostgreSQL provenance degrades to 'unverifiable-offline' (fail-closed for durable writes) rather than allow-by-default.
api_families.error_behavior: Denial returns a typed verdict with a calm reason and the failing clause; never a silent block.
api_families.rollback_behavior: A merge-gate/MinCut denial prevents a mutation from landing, so there is nothing to roll back; an isolated (severed) agent's in-flight work is discarded, not merged.
api_families.proof_behavior: Every verdict is proof-carrying: allow verdicts reference the satisfied capability grant; deny/verify verdicts reference the missing causal edge or witness root. This is the anti-bluffing guarantee (blueprint: mathematically impossible to write hallucinated fiction).
api_families.stability: v0; policy verdict schema stable; new policy clauses are additive and default to fail-closed for durable writes.

api_families.name: Canonical Data & Semantic Query API
api_families.purpose: The governed query surface over the canonical knowledge graph: hybrid semantic + relational search, knowledge-graph traversal, code AST/row reads, and causal-dependency edges. Richer than State-API projections — it is the human-visible analog of the agent Semantic-IDE (SQL + RuVector hybrid search). Reads are direct; writes are only via the Command API into BC-PERSIST.
api_families.transport: UDS / Tauri IPC query calls into BC-PERSIST (PostgreSQL + RuVector native extension, No Sidecar).

operations.name: hybrid_search
operations.routes_to: BC-PERSIST HybridSearch (ORDER BY semantic_embedding <-> [target]; BM25 + vector)

operations.name: traverse_graph
operations.routes_to: BC-PERSIST KnowledgeGraph (GNN causal edges, dependency columns)

operations.name: governed_write
operations.routes_to: REDIRECTS to API-COMMAND -> BC-PERSIST UPDATE under COW branching
operations.authority_owner: PostgreSQL; never a direct client write

api_families.authority_location: PostgreSQL + RuVector is the single durable owner of relational + semantic macro-state. The Data API is read + governed-write-via-Command only; RuVector runs as a native PostgreSQL extension (No Sidecar). redb performs f32 distance math only as a cache and holds no SQL/secondary index (STORE-001 REDB-CLAIM-008/009/010 refuted).
api_families.offline_behavior: Semantic/graph queries requiring live RuVector degrade to the last redb-cached vector distance results or a 'canonical-store-offline' typed response; the client never writes canonical data locally to compensate.
api_families.error_behavior: Query failure -> typed empty/degraded result with a calm reason; embeddings are reproducible (re-embed on recovery), so degraded reads are recoverable, not lost.
api_families.rollback_behavior: Reads have none; governed writes roll back via BC-PERSIST COW staging-branch discard (blueprint COW branching).
api_families.proof_behavior: Every returned code row/edge carries its provenance (source commit/PR lineage per adopted_capabilities.source.csv, module_path, witness root); results are traceable, not asserted.
api_families.stability: v0; query shapes stable; semantic-class full cutover to RuVector-in-PostgreSQL is gated on STORE-001 open dependency (live extension activation).

api_family_contract_coverage.purpose: Rigorous 'every X' enumeration: every governed published contract from CONSOLIDATE-001 (36 total across 15 contexts; the 2 BC-UI-PRESENTATION contracts define the client role and are excluded, leaving 34 consumed governed contracts) is fronted by at least one API family, and each is marked user-visible or internal-only. No governed contract is unreachable and no user-visible capability lacks a governed API.

coverage.note: read code rows / db_health; writes only via API-COMMAND
content-addressed capture; internal to ingestion, surfaced only as ingest progress
redb-backed live swarm status the UI renders
sync progress stream; write path itself is internal/forward-only
AI chat / ai_complete + token stream + agent status
.rvf hot-swap is runtime-internal; not directly client-driven
capability gating verdict; also internal on every command
SHAKE256 verification; surfaced as proof_ref, not a standalone screen
evidence corpus browse; producer is offline/internal
Yazelix toolchain env; build-time, not a user action surface
trigger musl static release + build log
orchestrates PortableBuild; surfaced via build log, not a separate control
token-minimizing CLI proxy; dev-side, not a UI screen
set/rotate key REFERENCES + view provenance refs; raw secrets never leave keyring

user_visible_capabilities.grounded_source: Blueprint: LifeOS renders swarm status by reading the same memory-mapped redb file via UDS

user_visible_capabilities.capability: Semantic search of the database-hosted codebase
user_visible_capabilities.grounded_source: Blueprint Semantic IDE: ORDER BY semantic_embedding <-> ; No-Sidecar RuVector
Blueprint: causal dependency edges, GNN in Postgres

user_visible_capabilities.authority_owner: AgentDB (.rvf) + provider; keys in BC-SECRET keyring
user_visible_capabilities.grounded_source: CLAUDE.md ai_complete dual-mode; Blueprint RuvLTRA Proportional Intelligence

user_visible_capabilities.authority_owner: redb transient tier; forward-only to PostgreSQL via envctl
user_visible_capabilities.grounded_source: Blueprint three-pillar ingestion: Nushell -> redb -> envctl -> PostgreSQL

user_visible_capabilities.authority_owner: envctl projection (read-only from PostgreSQL) + musl static build
user_visible_capabilities.grounded_source: Blueprint Phase 4 materialization + Nix musl install-in-place
Blueprint ATAS/ESN future timelines: state diffs, resource burn, failure nodes

user_visible_capabilities.authority_owner: subordinate task store; provenance references BC-PERSIST

user_visible_capabilities.capability: Accept a task handoff from the knowledge base
Browse / select a prompt from the registry
View policy verdicts, MinCut isolation, and merge gates
user_visible_capabilities.grounded_source: Blueprint MinCut immune system + agent guard PreToolUse denial (meta rules)

user_visible_capabilities.authority_owner: OS keyring + committed source pins; PostgreSQL stores references only
user_visible_capabilities.grounded_source: STORE-001 external-secret/provenance class; redb never holds secrets

user_visible_capabilities.capability: Meta multi-repo orchestration view (workspace tree, run status)
user_visible_capabilities.grounded_source: Blueprint gitkb/meta control plane; meta exec

user_visible_capabilities.capability: View GPU / hardware inventory for edge placement

offline.principle: STORE-001 single-durable-writer holds even offline: a client never becomes a durable authority. When PostgreSQL is offline or latent, redb is the application-level Write-Ahead Log and envctl forward-flushes on reconnect (forward-only).
offline.reads: State/Data reads degrade to last-known redb-buffered/cached snapshot with a 'stale as of <t>' marker; redb-backed status/streams keep flowing (local mmap/UDS).
offline.writes: Command dispatches needing a durable write are queued into the redb WAL and forward-flushed by envctl when PostgreSQL returns; the client is told 'queued, will sync', not that it owns the truth. Non-queueable commands (e.g. release build) are refused with a calm reason.
offline.grounded_source: Blueprint redb WAL: 'If Postgres ever goes offline... redb safely acts as an application-level Write-Ahead Log'; STORE-001 forward-only invariant

error.principle: Every API returns a typed Result; failures surface as calm, second-person, present-tense, sentence-case strings (design voice), never raw stack traces.
examples: 'LifeOS couldn't reach the AI provider right now — try again.' (Command API / AgentRuntime)
'LifeOS couldn't initialize storage at <path> — see logs.' (Application API boot)
error.structure: error_class + calm_message + optional proof_ref/log_ref; the client renders, it does not invent recovery.
error.grounded_source: CLAUDE.md AI-provider calm string + storage-init calm string + design voice contract

rollback.principle: Governed mutations are reversible through the OWNING context's mechanism; the client requests, the core executes. No client-side rollback of governed state.
mechanisms_by_context: BC-PERSIST / BC-ORCHESTRATION: COW staging-branch discard (blueprint COW branching)
BC-INGEST/BC-PERSIST cutover: BlobStore SHA-256 parity reversal, redb retained until PostgreSQL parity is green (STORE-001 verified-cutover invariant)
BC-CONTROL-PLANE: meta git snapshot restore for cross-repo operations
task-level: restore the archived interface-boundary plan; application source unchanged
rollback.handle: Every Command API result carries a rollback_handle bound to one of these mechanisms.
rollback.grounded_source: Blueprint COW branching; STORE-001 verified cutover; CLAUDE.md db_migrate idempotent; meta-destructive-commands snapshot restore

proof.principle: Anti-bluffing by construction: every governed mutation emits a SHAKE256 Cryptographic Witness Chain rooted in a PostgreSQL source vector; causal-graph verification rejects any claim without a historical root, so a client cannot forge a proof.
surfaces: Event API streams proof-carrying verdicts (MinCut isolation, merge-gate result)
Policy API verdicts reference the satisfied grant or the missing causal edge
planning-spine proof-record contract records task-level proof (this family emits proof_records/EXPERIENCE-001.proof.json)
proof.grounded_source: Blueprint Cryptographic Witness Chains (SHAKE256) + Causal Graph Verification; 'mathematically impossible to write hallucinated fiction'

api_stability.versioning: Semantic versioning per API family; current v0. Within a major version changes are additive-only (new operations, new optional fields, new event types).
api_stability.handshake: API-APPLICATION capability_handshake lets a client learn supported families + versions at attach time, so an older client keeps working when new families/operations are added.
api_stability.deprecation: Removals go through an explicit deprecation window; a client that ignores unknown event types / fields remains forward-compatible.
api_stability.transport_stability: Transport bindings (Tauri IPC, UDS, MessagePack subprocess, shared redb mmap) are part of the stable contract; the dual-mode window.__TAURI__ guard preserves the Vitest/browser canned path (CLAUDE.md).

transport_bindings.tauri_ipc: invoke() command/response + Tauri event channel — primary desktop binding (existing app: ai_complete, ai_provider_get/set, db_health, db_migrate).
transport_bindings.unix_domain_sockets: UDS request-response + shared memory-mapped redb reads for real-time status (blueprint: LifeOS connects via UDS / reads shared redb state files, avoiding HTTP/REST).
transport_bindings.messagepack_subprocess: typed plugin protocol over stdio (blueprint nu_plugin MessagePack; meta_plugin_protocol).
transport_bindings.note: All four are read/dispatch bindings into the Rust-native core; none let a client hold durable authority.

work_items.surface: API-APPLICATION — Application Lifecycle & Capability Handshake API
work_items.decision: Define the front-door API: boot/init (Storage::new -> migrate -> accounts migrate), capability/version handshake, db_health, navigate intents, shutdown (flush redb WAL forward). Core refuses to start on storage-init failure with the calm string. No domain authority lives here.

Define read-only projections over BC-ORCHESTRATION/BC-INGEST/BC-COGNITION/BC-GOVERNANCE/BC-DEVEX/BC-COMPUTE/BC-EVIDENCE/BC-PERSIST. No mutation path; reads degrade to redb-cached snapshots offline with a stale marker; projections carry provenance references.

Define the single mutation entry point: client dispatches a typed command, core validates via Policy API, the OWNING context executes under its STORE-001 authority, and returns result + proof_ref + rollback_handle. Offline durable writes queue into the redb WAL and forward-flush via envctl. Preserve the dual-mode window.__TAURI__ path.

Define server-pushed read-only streams (swarm status, AI tokens, ingest progress, build log, forecast updates, policy/MinCut/merge verdicts) over UDS + shared redb tail. redb-sourced streams survive PostgreSQL outage; verdict events are proof-carrying. A subscription cannot mutate.

Define the governed extension surface: list/invoke/register typed subprocess plugins over MessagePack and nu_plugin CodeDB ingestion. Plugin capabilities are core-granted and Policy-gated; nu_plugin ingestion buffers into redb and forward-bridges to PostgreSQL, never becoming a durable authority.

Define the enforcement seam over BC-INTEGRITY: evaluate_policy, check_merge_gate, verify_witness, report_immune_status. Verdicts are advisory to clients but binding in the core (Command API re-checks; a client cannot bypass). Fail-closed for durable writes when verification is offline.

work_items.surface: API-DATA — Canonical Data & Semantic Query API
work_items.decision: Define the governed query surface over BC-PERSIST (hybrid_search, traverse_graph, read_code_rows); governed writes redirect to API-COMMAND under COW branching. PostgreSQL + RuVector is the single durable owner (No Sidecar); redb is distance-math cache only. Semantic-class full cutover gated on STORE-001 RuVector-in-PostgreSQL activation.

Pin the Rust-native-core vs replaceable-client line: clients render, dispatch, subscribe, request verdicts, display proofs; clients may NOT mutate durable state, embed business logic, become a durable writer, hold raw secrets, bypass policy, or re-derive authority. The six workspaces + settings are navigation groupings with no authority. Any client (Vue3+Tauri now; desktop/mobile/glasses/ambient later) is swappable without moving logic.
fronts_bounded_contexts: all 14 governed contexts (BC-UI-PRESENTATION is the client role)

work_items.surface: Cross-cutting offline / error / rollback / proof behavior
work_items.decision: Specify the four cross-cutting behaviors globally and per family: offline (redb WAL + forward-only flush; client never a durable authority), error (typed Results + calm design-voice strings), rollback (owning-context mechanisms: COW discard / idempotent migration / SHA-256 parity reversal / meta snapshot restore; client requests, core executes), proof (SHAKE256 witness chains rooted in PostgreSQL vectors + causal-graph verification; proof_ref on every mutation).

Define stability: semantic-versioned families (v0), additive-only within a major, capability handshake for forward compatibility, explicit deprecation windows. Fix transport bindings (Tauri IPC, UDS, MessagePack subprocess, shared redb mmap) as part of the stable contract; preserve the dual-mode window.__TAURI__ guard so Vitest/browser paths stay green.

dependency_ordering_rationale: H first pins the client/authority boundary that every family obeys. A (application front door + handshake) and F (policy enforcement) precede the domain families because every command and plugin invocation is policy-gated at attach time. Read families (B state, G data) precede the mutation family (C command) because commands return projections that reads must already model. D (events) and E (plugins) build on the command/read surfaces. I and J are cross-cutting closures over the finished family set.

clause_1_every_user_visible_capability_r.evidence: user_visible_capabilities enumerates 19 capabilities, each mapped to one or more of the seven API families and to exactly one governing bounded context + published contract from CONSOLIDATE-001. api_family_contract_coverage proves the inverse: all 34 consumed governed contracts are fronted by an API family (27 user-visible, 7 internal-only), so no user-visible capability lacks a governed API and no governed contract is unreachable. The seven families exactly match the seven API kinds named in the task goal (application, state, command, event, plugin, policy, data).

clause_2_business_logic_and_authority_st.evidence: application_boundary.client_contract lists clients_may (render/dispatch/subscribe/request-verdict/display) vs clients_may_not (mutate durable state, embed business rules, become a durable writer, hold raw secrets, bypass policy, re-derive authority). Every API family names an authority_location inside a Rust-native bounded context; the Command API routes mutations into the OWNING context and the Policy API re-checks so a client cannot bypass. Aligns with STORE-001: BC-UI-PRESENTATION owns no durable macro-state and PostgreSQL remains the single durable writer. The six workspaces + settings are declared navigation groupings with no authority.

clause_3_offline_error_rollback_and_proo.evidence: cross_cutting_behavior specifies all four globally AND each api_families entry specifies its own offline_behavior/error_behavior/rollback_behavior/proof_behavior. Offline = redb application-level WAL + envctl forward-only flush (client never a durable authority). Error = typed Results + calm design-voice strings (two verbatim examples). Rollback = owning-context mechanisms (COW discard, idempotent migration, SHA-256 parity reversal, meta snapshot restore) executed by the core on client request. Proof = SHAKE256 witness chains rooted in PostgreSQL source vectors + causal-graph verification + proof_ref on every mutation.

store_001_alignment.note: The API boundary enforces STORE-001 by construction. No API family lets a client mutate durable state; the Command/Data APIs route every durable write into the owning context, where PostgreSQL + RuVector is the single durable owner of relational + semantic macro-state, redb is the transient/WAL tier (offline queue is forward-only), AgentDB owns per-agent cognition, envctl owns derived projections, and the OS keyring owns secrets (references only in the client). BC-UI-PRESENTATION and every future client own no durable macro-state.
store_001_alignment.verified_cutover_respected: The Command/Plugin APIs surface ingestion into the redb buffer and forward-bridge to PostgreSQL; durable-class cutover happens only via the verified BlobStore SHA-256 parity contract server-side, never by a client assumption.

rollback: Restore the archived interface-boundary plan; application source remains unchanged. No API is implemented, no client is rewritten, no deployment occurs — this family defines the boundary and API contract only.
open_dependencies: EXPERIENCE-002 decomposes the concrete desktop/mobile/glasses/ambient client families that consume this seven-family API (LIFEOS-010).
EXPERIENCE-003 defines cross-interface continuity + accessibility acceptance over the same governed API set.
Semantic-class (API-DATA) full cutover to RuVector-in-PostgreSQL is gated on STORE-001 open dependency: live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation (NBVERIFY-001 remainder).
Durable-class (API-COMMAND/DATA) cutover proof is gated on STORE-001 open dependency: live blobstore_parity.rs green run against a disposable PostgreSQL.
blocked_paths_respected: No application source mutated, no UI rewritten, no deployment, no secrets touched. Only planning artifacts under generated/** and proof_records/** are written.
completion_gate: All seven API families (application, state, command, event, plugin, policy, data) are specified with operations, authority_location, and per-family offline/error/rollback/proof behavior; every one of the 19 user-visible capabilities maps to a governed API family + exactly one governing context/contract; all 34 consumed governed contracts are fronted (coverage complete); the client contract keeps business logic and durable authority server-side (STORE-001 single-durable-writer preserved); and proof_records/EXPERIENCE-001.proof.json records the three gate clauses satisfied with concrete values and product_execution_performed=false.
